concepts.name: AuditEventType
concepts.what: Discriminated event types recorded in the audit trail.

concepts.name: AuditEvent
concepts.what: A single audit event recorded in the forge-ts audit trail.

properties.name: timestamp
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the event occurred.

properties.name: user
properties.description: OS username of the actor (falls back to "unknown").

properties.name: reason
properties.description: Mandatory for lock/unlock/bypass events; optional otherwise.

concepts.name: ReadAuditOptions
concepts.what: Options for reading the audit log.

properties.name: limit
properties.description: Maximum number of events to return.

properties.name: eventType
properties.description: Filter to a single event type.

concepts.name: BypassConfig
concepts.what: Configuration for the bypass budget system.

properties.name: dailyBudget
properties.description: Maximum number of bypasses allowed per calendar day. Default: 3

properties.name: durationHours
properties.description: Duration in hours before a bypass automatically expires. Default: 24

concepts.name: BypassRecord
concepts.what: A single bypass record stored in `.forge-bypass.json`.

properties.name: createdAt
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the bypass was created.

properties.name: expiresAt
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the bypass expires.

properties.name: reason
properties.description: Mandatory justification for why the bypass was created.

properties.name: rule
properties.description: Specific rule code bypassed (e.g., "E009"), or "all" for a blanket bypass.

properties.name: user
properties.description: OS username of the actor who created the bypass.

concepts.name: ForgeConfig
concepts.what: Full configuration for a forge-ts run. Loaded from forge-ts.config.ts or the "forge-ts" key in package.json.

properties.name: tsconfig
properties.description: Path to the tsconfig.json to compile against.

properties.name: enforce

type.original: { enabled: boolean; minVisibility: Visibility | "public" | "beta" | "internal" | "private"; strict: boolean; rules: EnforceRules; ignoreFile?: string; }

properties.description: Enforce TSDoc on all public exports.

type.original: { enabled: boolean; openapi: boolean; openapiPath: string; }
{ enabled: boolean; formats: Array<"markdown" | "mdx">; llmsTxt: boolean; readmeSync: boolean; ssgTarget?: "docusaurus" | "mintlify" | "nextra" | "vitepress"; ckm?: boolean; }

properties.name: skill

type.original: { enabled?: boolean; customSections?: Array<{ heading: string; content: string; }>; extraGotchas?: string[]; }

properties.description: Skill package generation settings. Custom sections here are merged into the generated SKILL.md, allowing projects to inject workflow knowledge, domain gotchas, and other context that cannot be derived from symbols alone.

type.original: { writeConfig: boolean; customTags: Array<{ tagName: string; syntaxKind: "block" | "inline" | "modifier"; }>; enforce: { core: "error" | "warn" | "off"; extended: "error" | "warn" | "off"; discretionary: "error" | "warn" | "off"; }; }

properties.name: bypass
properties.description: Bypass budget configuration for temporary rule overrides.

type.original: { enabled: boolean; autoDiscover: boolean; custom: Array<{ slug: string; title: string; sources: string[]; }>; }

{ tsconfig: { enabled: boolean; requiredFlags: string[]; }; biome: { enabled: boolean; lockedRules: string[]; }; packageJson: { enabled: boolean; minNodeVersion: string; requiredFields: string[]; }; }

properties.name: _configWarnings
properties.description: Warnings generated during config loading (e.g., unknown keys). Populated by loadConfig(). Agents should surface these in output.

properties.name: project

type.original: { repository?: string; homepage?: string; packageName?: string; description?: string; version?: string; bin?: Record<string, string>; scripts?: Record<string, string>; keywords?: string[]; }

properties.description: Project metadata — auto-detected from package.json if not provided.

concepts.name: ForgeResult
concepts.what: The result of a forge-ts compilation pass.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether the run succeeded without errors.

properties.name: symbols
properties.description: All symbols extracted during this run.

properties.name: errors
properties.description: Errors that caused or would cause failure.

properties.name: duration
properties.description: Wall-clock duration of the run in milliseconds.

properties.name: writtenFiles
properties.description: Absolute paths of files written during this run (populated by gen).

concepts.name: ReferenceEntry
concepts.what: A single entry in the generated API reference.

type.original: { name: string; description: string; type?: string; }[] | undefined

properties.name: children
properties.description: Nested child symbols (class methods, interface properties, enum members).

concepts.name: ForgeLoggerOptions
concepts.what: Options for configuring the forge-ts logger at CLI startup.

properties.name: json
properties.description: JSON output mode (--json flag). LAFS handles JSON; suppress consola.

concepts.name: AuditResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `audit` command.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether the audit log was read successfully.

concepts.name: BarometerResult
concepts.what: Full barometer output written to `.forge/barometer.json`.

properties.name: project
properties.description: Project name from package.json or config.

properties.name: generated
properties.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of when the barometer was generated.

properties.name: symbolCount
properties.description: Total number of exported symbols analyzed.

properties.name: rubric
properties.description: Scoring rubric for evaluating documentation effectiveness.

properties.name: instructions
properties.description: Agent instructions — present only in `--questions-only` output.

concepts.name: BarometerScoreResult
concepts.what: Full barometer score output.

concepts.name: FrontmatterResult
concepts.what: Result of parsing frontmatter from markdown/MDX content.

properties.name: body
properties.description: The body content without the frontmatter block.

properties.name: data
properties.description: The parsed frontmatter data as a key-value map.

concepts.name: SiteGeneratorOptions
concepts.what: Options controlling the doc site generator.

properties.name: packageName
properties.description: npm package name for install commands.

concepts.name: CKMConfigEntry
concepts.what: A single entry in the configuration schema.

concepts.name: MarkdownOptions
concepts.what: Options controlling Markdown output.

properties.name: mdx
properties.description: Whether to use MDX syntax (default: Markdown).

concepts.name: ReadmeSyncOptions
concepts.what: Options controlling README sync behaviour.

properties.name: badge
properties.description: Include a "Documented with forge-ts" badge above the API table.

concepts.name: SSGConfigFile
concepts.what: A single generated SSG configuration file.

concepts.name: GenerateOptions
concepts.what: Options for the generation pipeline.

properties.name: forceStubs
properties.description: When true, overwrite stub pages even if they already exist on disk. Normally stub pages (concepts, guides, faq, contributing, changelog) are only created on the first build to preserve manual edits. Use this to reset stubs to their scaffolding state.

concepts.name: BuildResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `build` command.

type.original: { steps: number; succeeded: number; failed: number; duration: number; }

properties.name: generatedFiles
properties.description: Files written during the build — present at standard and full MVI levels.

concepts.name: BypassCreateResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `bypass` command when creating a bypass.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether the bypass was successfully created.

properties.name: bypass
properties.description: The bypass record that was created.

properties.name: remainingBudget
properties.description: Number of remaining bypass slots for today after creation.

concepts.name: BypassStatusResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `bypass --status` command.

properties.name: remainingBudget
properties.description: Number of remaining bypass slots for today.

properties.name: expiredRemoved
properties.description: Number of expired bypasses that were cleaned up.

concepts.name: FormatOptions
concepts.what: Options that control how `formatResults` renders its output.

properties.name: colors
properties.description: Emit ANSI colour escape sequences when `true`.

properties.name: verbose
properties.description: When `true`, include the symbol's type signature alongside each diagnostic so the reader has immediate context.

concepts.name: CheckResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `check` command.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether the check passed without errors.

properties.name: summary

type.original: { errors: number; warnings: number; files: number; symbols: number; duration: number; }

properties.description: Aggregate counts — always present regardless of MVI level.

properties.name: triage
properties.description: Triage data for prioritizing fixes — present when errors  0 (except minimal).

properties.name: byFile
properties.description: Per-file breakdown — present at standard and full MVI levels, paginated.

properties.name: page
properties.description: Pagination metadata when byFile is paginated.

properties.name: filters
properties.description: Active filters applied to this result.

properties.name: nextCommand
properties.description: CLI command hint for the agent to run next.

concepts.name: InitHooksResult
concepts.what: Result of the `init hooks` command.

properties.name: hookManager
properties.description: The detected or chosen hook manager.

properties.name: files
properties.description: Relative paths of all files written.

concepts.name: InitProjectResult
concepts.what: Result of the `init` (project setup) command.

properties.name: skipped
properties.description: Files that already existed and were skipped.

properties.name: scriptsAdded
properties.description: Scripts that were added to package.json (empty if none were added).

concepts.name: DoctorCheckStatus
concepts.what: Status of a single doctor check.

concepts.name: DoctorCheckResult
concepts.what: Result of a single doctor check.

properties.name: fixable
properties.description: Whether this issue can be auto-fixed with --fix.

concepts.name: DoctorResult
concepts.what: Result of the `doctor` command.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether all checks passed without errors.

type.original: { passed: number; warnings: number; errors: number; info: number; }

properties.name: fixed
properties.description: Files that were fixed (only populated when --fix is used).

concepts.name: InitDocsResult
concepts.what: Result of the `init docs` command.

properties.name: target
properties.description: The SSG target that was scaffolded.

type.original: { filesCreated: number; dependencies: number; scripts: number; }

properties.name: files
properties.description: Relative paths of all files created.

concepts.name: LockResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `lock` command.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether the lock was successfully created.

properties.name: lockFile
properties.description: Path to the lock file that was written.

properties.name: lockedAt
properties.description: ISO-8601 timestamp when the lock was created.

properties.name: lockedBy
properties.description: Identifier of who created the lock.

type.original: { rules: number; tsconfig: boolean; biome: boolean; }

properties.name: overwrote
properties.description: Whether a previous lock file was overwritten.

concepts.name: PrepublishResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `prepublish` command.

properties.name: success
properties.description: Whether both check and build passed.

type.original: { steps: number; passed: number; failed: number; duration: number; }
{ success: boolean; errors: number; warnings: number; duration: number; }

properties.name: build

type.original: { success: boolean; steps: number; succeeded: number; failed: number; duration: number; } | undefined

properties.description: Result of the build step (absent if check failed and build was skipped).

properties.name: skippedReason
properties.description: If check failed, the reason build was skipped.

concepts.name: GeneratorOptions
concepts.what: Options for virtual test file generation.

properties.name: cacheDir
properties.description: Directory where virtual test files will be written.

concepts.name: RunResult
concepts.what: Result of running the generated test files.

properties.name: output
properties.description: Combined stdout + stderr output from the test runner.

properties.name: tests
properties.description: Individual test results with name and status.

concepts.name: TestCaseResult
concepts.what: The result of a single test case.

properties.name: name
properties.description: The full test name as reported by the runner.

properties.name: sourceFile
properties.description: The source file this test was generated from, if determinable.

concepts.name: TestResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `test` command.

properties.name: summary

type.original: { passed: number; failed: number; total: number; duration: number; }

properties.description: Aggregate counts — always present regardless of MVI level.

properties.name: failures
properties.description: Per-failure details — present at standard and full MVI levels.

concepts.name: UnlockResult
concepts.what: Typed result for the `unlock` command.

properties.name: previousLockedBy
properties.description: Who originally locked the config, if known.

properties.name: previousLockedAt
properties.description: When the config was originally locked, if known.

operations.name: createBypass
operations.what: Creates a new bypass record, writes it to `.forge-bypass.json`, and appends an audit event.
preconditions: Error when the daily bypass budget is exhausted.

inputs.name: rootDir
inputs.description: Absolute path to the project root directory.

inputs.name: rule
inputs.description: Specific rule code to bypass (e.g., "E009"), or "all". Defaults to "all".

operations.name: createLockManifest
operations.what: Creates a `ForgeLockManifest` from the current project config.

inputs.name: lockedBy
inputs.description: Identifier of the user or agent creating the lock. Defaults to `"forge-ts lock"`.

type.original: A new lock manifest ready to be written with `writeLockFile`.

outputs.description: A new lock manifest ready to be written with `writeLockFile`.

operations.name: validateAgainstLock
operations.what: Validates the current config against a locked manifest.

inputs.name: lock
inputs.description: The lock manifest to validate against.

type.original: An array of `LockViolation` entries. Empty means no weakening detected.

outputs.description: An array of `LockViolation` entries. Empty means no weakening detected.

operations.name: createWalker
operations.what: Creates an `ASTWalker` configured for the given forge config.

type.original: An `ASTWalker` instance whose `walk()` method performs the extraction.

outputs.description: An `ASTWalker` instance whose `walk()` method performs the extraction.

operations.name: generateOpenAPISpec
operations.what: Generates a production-quality OpenAPI 3.2 document from the extracted SDK types.  The document is populated with: - An `info` block sourced from the config or reasonable defaults. - A `components.schemas` section with one schema per exported type. - `tags` derived from unique source file paths (grouping by file). - Visibility filtering: `@internal` symbols are never emitted. - HTTP paths are extracted from `@route` tags and appropriately mapped.

inputs.name: sdkTypes
inputs.description: SDK types to include as component schemas.

inputs.name: symbols
inputs.description: Raw symbols used to extract HTTP route paths from `@route` tags.

operations.name: buildReference
operations.what: Builds a structured API reference from a list of exported symbols.  Unlike the minimal stub, this version includes nested children (class methods, interface properties) and all available TSDoc metadata.  Symbols with `Visibility.Internal` or `Visibility.Private` are excluded from the top-level results. Children with private/internal visibility are also filtered out.

inputs.name: symbols
inputs.description: All symbols from the AST walker.

type.original: An array of `ReferenceEntry` objects sorted by name.

outputs.description: An array of `ReferenceEntry` objects sorted by name.

operations.name: generateApi
operations.what: Runs the API generation pipeline: walk → extract → generate → write.

type.original: A `ForgeResult` with success/failure and any diagnostics.

outputs.description: A `ForgeResult` with success/failure and any diagnostics.

operations.name: runAudit
operations.what: Reads the audit log and returns a typed command output.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the audit command.

operations.name: auditCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts audit`.

operations.name: runBarometer
operations.what: Runs the barometer generation pass.  Loads the project config, walks the symbol graph, extracts testable facts from five categories, generates questions with ground-truth answers, and writes the result to `.forge/barometer.json`.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the barometer command.

operations.name: runBarometerScore
operations.what: Scores agent answers against the barometer answer key.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments including project root and path to agent answers file.

operations.name: barometerCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts barometer`.  Generates a documentation effectiveness test (questions + answers + rubric) from the project's source code. Includes a `score` subcommand for evaluating agent answers.

operations.name: generateDocSite
operations.what: Generates a full multi-page documentation site from symbols grouped by package.  Follows a 5-stage information architecture: 1. ORIENT — Landing page, Getting Started 2. LEARN — Concepts (stub) 3. BUILD — Guides (stub) 4. REFERENCE — API Reference, Types, Configuration, Changelog 5. COMMUNITY — FAQ, Contributing (stubs)

type.original: An array of `DocPage` objects ready to be written to disk.

outputs.description: An array of `DocPage` objects ready to be written to disk.

operations.name: generateCKM
operations.what: Generates a Codebase Knowledge Manifest from the symbol graph.

inputs.name: symbols
inputs.description: All symbols extracted from the project.

type.original: A complete `CKMManifest` ready for JSON serialization.

outputs.description: A complete `CKMManifest` ready for JSON serialization.

operations.name: generateLlmsTxt
operations.what: Generates an `llms.txt` routing manifest from the extracted symbols.  The file follows the llms.txt specification: a compact, structured overview designed to help large language models navigate a project's documentation. Symbols are grouped by package with  summaries.

type.original: The generated `llms.txt` content as a string.

outputs.description: The generated `llms.txt` content as a string.

operations.name: generateLlmsFullTxt
operations.what: Generates an `llms-full.txt` dense context file from the extracted symbols.  Unlike `llms.txt`, this file contains complete documentation for every exported symbol, intended for LLM ingestion that requires full context. Symbols are grouped by package with  summaries.

type.original: The generated `llms-full.txt` content as a string.

outputs.description: The generated `llms-full.txt` content as a string.

operations.name: generateMarkdown
operations.what: Generates a Markdown (or MDX) string from a list of symbols.

operations.name: generateSkillPackage
operations.what: Generates an agentskills.io-compliant skill package for ANY TypeScript project.  All content is derived from the project's exported symbols and metadata. No hardcoded project-specific content. Works for any project that forge-ts analyzes.

type.original: A `SkillPackage` describing the directory and its files.

outputs.description: A `SkillPackage` describing the directory and its files.

operations.name: generateSkillMd
operations.what: Generates a SKILL.md string following the Agent Skills specification. Generic for any TypeScript project — content derived from symbols.

type.original: The SKILL.md content as a string.

outputs.description: The SKILL.md content as a string.

operations.name: generateSSGConfigs
operations.what: Generate navigation configuration file(s) for the given SSG target.  Returns one file for most targets, but multiple files for Nextra (which uses per-directory `_meta.json` files).

inputs.name: projectName
inputs.description: The project name (used in config metadata).

type.original: An array of `SSGConfigFile` objects ready to be written to disk.

outputs.description: An array of `SSGConfigFile` objects ready to be written to disk.

operations.name: generate
operations.what: Runs the full generation pipeline: walk → render → write.  Auto-generated pages are always regenerated from source code. Stub pages (scaffolding for human/agent editing) are only created if they don't already exist, preserving manual edits across builds. Pass `{ forceStubs: true }` to overwrite stubs.

operations.name: runBuild
operations.what: Runs the full build pipeline and returns a typed command output.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the build command.

operations.name: buildCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts build`.

operations.name: runBypassCreate
operations.what: Runs the bypass creation: creates a new bypass record with budget enforcement.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the bypass command.

operations.name: runBypassStatus
operations.what: Runs the bypass status query: shows active bypasses and remaining budget.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the bypass status command.

operations.name: bypassCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts bypass`.

operations.name: enforce
operations.what: Runs the TSDoc enforcement pass against a project.  The enforcer walks all exported symbols that meet the configured minimum visibility threshold and emits diagnostics for any documentation deficiencies it finds.  ### Error codes | Code | Severity | Condition | |------|----------|-----------| | E001 | error    | Exported symbol is missing a TSDoc summary. | | E002 | error    | Function/method parameter lacks a `@param` tag. | | E003 | error    | Non-void function/method lacks a `@returns` tag. | | E004 | error    | Exported function/method is missing an `@example` block. | | E005 | error    | Package entry point (index.ts) is missing `@packageDocumentation`. | | E006 | error    | Public/protected class member is missing a TSDoc comment. | | E007 | error    | Interface/type alias property is missing a TSDoc comment. | | W001 | warning  | TSDoc comment contains parse errors. | | W002 | warning  | Function body throws but has no `@throws` tag. | | W003 | warning  | `@deprecated` tag is present without explanation. | | W006 | warning  | TSDoc parser-level syntax error (invalid tag, malformed block, etc.). | | E009 | error    | tsconfig.json required strict-mode flag is missing or disabled (guard). | | E010 | error    | Config drift: a rule severity is weaker than the locked value. | | E013 | error    | Exported function/class is missing a `@remarks` block. | | E014 | warn     | Optional property of interface/type is missing `@defaultValue`. | | E015 | error    | Generic symbol is missing `@typeParam` for a type parameter. | | W005 | warn     | Symbol references other symbols via `{@link}` but has no `@see` tags. | | W007 | warn     | Guide FORGE:AUTO section references a symbol that no longer exists. | | W008 | warn     | Exported public symbol is not mentioned in any guide page. | | E017 | error    | `@internal` symbol re-exported through public barrel (index.ts). | | E018 | warn     | `@route`-tagged function missing `@response` tag. | | W009 | warn     | `{@inheritDoc}` references a symbol that does not exist. | | W010 | warn     | `@breaking` tag present without `@migration` path. | | W011 | warn     | New public export missing `@since` version tag. | | E019 | error    | Non-test file contains `@ts-expect-error` / `@ts-expect-error`. | | E020 | error    | Exported symbol has `any` in its public API signature. | | W012 | warn     | `{@link}` display text appears stale relative to target summary. | | W013 | warn     | `@example` block may be stale (arg count mismatch). | | W014 | warn     | `@param` name in TSDoc doesn't match actual parameter name. | | W015 | warn     | `@param` count in TSDoc doesn't match actual parameter count. | | W016 | warn     | `@returns` tag on a void/Promise function. | | W017 | warn     | `@remarks` block is empty or contains only placeholder text. | | W018 | warn     | `@operation`-tagged function missing required CKM docs (`@param`, `@returns`, `@remarks`, `@example`). | | W019 | warn     | CKM tag (`@operation`, `@constraint`, `@workflow`, `@concept`) has insufficient content. | | W020 | warn     | `@constraint`-tagged symbol missing `@throws` to document constraint violation. |  When `config.enforce.strict` is `true` all warnings are promoted to errors.

type.original: A `ForgeResult` describing which symbols passed or failed.

outputs.description: A `ForgeResult` describing which symbols passed or failed.

operations.name: runCheck
operations.what: Runs the TSDoc enforcement pass and returns a typed command output.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the check command.

operations.name: checkCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts check`.

operations.name: runDocsDev
operations.what: Starts the local dev server for the configured SSG target.  Reads `gen.ssgTarget` from the forge-ts config, resolves the adapter, and spawns the platform's dev server in the output directory.

type.original: A promise that resolves when the server exits.

outputs.description: A promise that resolves when the server exits.

operations.name: docsDevCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts docs dev`.

operations.name: generateHuskyHook
operations.what: Generates the husky pre-commit hook file content (modern husky v9+).

operations.name: generateHuskyPrePushHook
operations.what: Generates the husky pre-push hook file content (modern husky v9+).

operations.name: generateLefthookBlock
operations.what: Generates the lefthook block with both pre-commit and pre-push sections.

operations.name: runInitHooks
operations.what: Scaffolds git hook integration for the project.  Detects the hook manager (husky or lefthook), generates appropriate hook files, and reports what was written.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the init hooks command.

operations.name: initHooksCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts init hooks`.  Scaffolds git hook integration for the project by detecting the hook manager (husky or lefthook) and generating pre-commit hooks.

operations.name: buildTsdocContent
operations.what: Builds tsdoc.json content, merging in customTags from the forge config.  When `customTags` is non-empty, the written tsdoc.json includes both `tagDefinitions` and `supportForTags` entries so that the TSDoc parser (and any editor extensions) recognise the custom tags.

operations.name: runInitProject
operations.what: Runs the full project init flow.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the init command.

operations.name: initProjectCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts init` (bare — full project setup).  Detects the project environment, writes default configuration files, validates tsconfig/package.json, and reports a summary.

operations.name: runDoctor
operations.what: Runs the doctor integrity check flow.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the doctor command.

operations.name: doctorCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts doctor`.  Performs project integrity checks and optionally auto-fixes resolvable issues with `--fix`.

operations.name: runInitDocs
operations.what: Scaffolds a documentation site for the target SSG platform.  Resolves the target from args, validates it, checks for an existing scaffold, calls the adapter's `scaffold()` method, and writes all files produced by the manifest to `outDir`.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the init docs command.

operations.name: initDocsCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts init docs`.  Scaffolds a complete documentation site for the target SSG platform. Use `--json` for LAFS JSON envelope output (agent/CI-friendly).

operations.name: initCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts init`.  Exposes subcommands for scaffolding project artefacts.

operations.name: runLock
operations.what: Runs the lock command: reads current config and creates `.forge-lock.json`.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the lock command.

operations.name: lockCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts lock`.

operations.name: runPrepublish
operations.what: Runs the prepublish safety gate: check then build.  If the check step fails, the build step is skipped entirely. Both steps use the same project root (cwd).

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the prepublish command.

operations.name: prepublishCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts prepublish`.  Runs check then build as a publish safety gate. Add to package.json as: `"prepublishOnly": "forge-ts prepublish"`

operations.name: generateTestFiles
operations.what: Generates a virtual test file for a set of extracted examples.  Each example is wrapped in an `it()` block using the Node built-in `node:test` runner so that no additional test framework is required. Auto-imports the tested symbol from the source file, processes `// =>` assertion patterns, and appends an inline source map.

inputs.name: examples
inputs.description: Examples to include in the generated file.

type.original: An array of `VirtualTestFile` objects (one per source file).

outputs.description: An array of `VirtualTestFile` objects (one per source file).

operations.name: runTests
operations.what: Writes virtual test files to disk and executes them with Node 24 native TypeScript support (`--experimental-strip-types --test`).

inputs.name: files
inputs.description: The virtual test files to write and run.

operations.name: runTest
operations.what: Runs the doctest pipeline and returns a typed command output.

inputs.name: args
inputs.description: CLI arguments for the test command.

operations.name: testCommand
operations.what: Citty command definition for `forge-ts test`.

operations.name: runUnlock

When true, overwrite stub pages even if they already exist on disk. Normally stub pages (concepts, guides, faq, contributing, changelog) are only created on the first build to preserve manual edits. Use this to reset stubs to their scaffolding state.
